name: dag-cbor

metadata.name: dag-cbor
metadata.summary: Python implementation of the DAG-CBOR codec.

documentation:
# dag-cbor: A Python implementation of the [DAG-CBOR codec](https://ipld.io/specs/codecs/dag-cbor/spec/)

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.7+-green.svg
:alt: Python versions
:target: https://docs.python.org/3.7/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/dag-cbor.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/dag-cbor/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/dag-cbor.svg
:alt: PyPI status
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/dag-cbor/
```

```{image} http://www.mypy-lang.org/static/mypy_badge.svg
:alt: Checked with Mypy
:target: https://github.com/python/mypy
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/dag-cbor/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://dag-cbor.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://github.com/hashberg-io/dag-cbor/actions/workflows/python-pytest.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Python package status
:target: https://github.com/hashberg-io/dag-cbor/actions/workflows/python-pytest.yml
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/readme%20style-standard-brightgreen.svg?style=flat-square
:alt: standard-readme compliant
:target: https://github.com/RichardLitt/standard-readme
```

This is a fully compliant Python implementation of the [DAG-CBOR codec](https://ipld.io/specs/codecs/dag-cbor/spec/), a subset of the [Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR)](https://cbor.io/) supporting the [IPLD Data Model](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/) and enforcing a unique (strict) encoded representation of items.

```{contents}
```

## Install

You can install the latest release from [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/dag-cbor/) as follows:

```console
$ pip install --upgrade dag-cbor
```

## Usage

We suggest you import DAG-CBOR as follows:

```pycon
>>> import dag_cbor
```

Below are some basic usage examples, to get you started: for detailed documentation, see <https://dag-cbor.readthedocs.io/>

### Encoding and decoding

```pycon
>>> dag_cbor.encode({'a': 12, 'b': 'hello!'})
b'\xa2aa\x0cabfhello!'
>>> dag_cbor.decode(b'\xa2aa\x0cabfhello!')
{'a': 12, 'b': 'hello!'}
```

### Random DAG-CBOR data

```pycon
>>> import pprint # pretty-printing
>>> custom_opts = dict(min_codepoint=0x41, max_codepoint=0x5a, include_cid=False)
>>> with dag_cbor.random.options(**custom_opts):
...     for d in dag_cbor.random.rand_dict(3):
...             pprint.pp(d)
...
{'BIQPMZ': b'\x85\x1f\x07/\xcc\x00\xfc\xaa',
 'EJEYDTZI': {},
 'PLSG': {'G': 'JFG',
          'HZE': -61.278,
          'JWDRKRGZ': b'-',
          'OCCKQPDJ': True,
          'SJOCTZMK': False},
 'PRDLN': 39.129,
 'TUGRP': None,
 'WZTEJDXC': -69.933}
{'GHAXI': 39.12,
 'PVUWZLC': 4.523,
 'TDPSU': 'TVCADUGT',
 'ZHGVSNSI': [-57, 9, -78.312]}
{'': 11, 'B': True, 'FWD': {}, 'GXZBVAR': 'BTDWMGI', 'TDICHC': 87}
```

## API

For the full API documentation, see <https://dag-cbor.readthedocs.io/>

## Contributing

Please see [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

modules.name: dag_cbor.encoding
documentation: Encoding functions for DAG-CBOR codec.

functions.name: dag_cbor.encoding.check_key_compliance
documentation: Enforces DAG-CBOR compliance for keys in a mapping.

functions.name: dag_cbor.encoding.canonical_order_dict
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with canonically ordered keys, according to the DAG-CBOR specification.
Specifically, keys are sorted increasingly first by length and then by the lexicographic ordering of the corresponding UTF-8 bytestrings.

functions.name: dag_cbor.encoding.encode
documentation:
Encodes the given data with the DAG-CBOR codec.

By default, the encoded data is written to an internal stream and the bytes are returned at the end (as a `bytes` object).

```python
def encode(data: IPLDKind, stream: None = None) -> bytes:
    ...
```

Example usage:

```pycon
>>> dag_cbor.encode({'a': 12, 'b': 'hello!'})
b'\xa2aa\x0cabfhello!'
```

If a `stream` is given, the encoded data is written to the stream and the number of bytes written is returned:

```python
def encode(data: IPLDKind, stream: BufferedIOBase) -> int:
    ...
```

Example usage with a stream:

```pycon
>>> from io import BytesIO
>>> stream = BytesIO()
>>> dag_cbor.encode({'a': 12, 'b': 'hello!'}, stream=stream)
13
>>> stream.getvalue()
b'\xa2aa\x0cabfhello!'
```

```
param data

the DAG data to be encoded

param stream

an optional stream into which the encoded data should be written

param include_multicodec

if , the encoded data is prefixed by the multicodec code for 'dag-cbor'
(see multicodec.wrap).

param normalize_strings

whether strings should be normalised prior to encoding

raises CBOREncodingError

if an  outside of range(-2**64, 2**64) is encountered

raises DAGCBOREncodingError

if a value of type other than , , , , ,
, , , or  is encountered

raises DAGCBOREncodingError

if attempting to encode the special  values NaN, Infinity and -Infinity

raises DAGCBOREncodingError

if a key of a dictionary is not a string
```

functions.name: dag_cbor.encoding._check_key_compliance
documentation: Check keys for DAG-CBOR compliance.

modules.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err
documentation: Detailed messages for all possible DAG-CBOR decoding errors.

modules.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils
documentation: Utility functions used to produce messages for DAG-CBOR decoding errors.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils._bytes2hex
documentation: Converts bytes to a hex string, showing a truncated string if the number of bytes exceeds 16.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils._decode_error_msg_lines
documentation:
This utility function takes one or more stream snapshots as input and collates the chunks of read bytes. Let `bs` be the bytes read across the
snapshots (which are assumed to be sequential) and `pos` be the position in the stream of the first byte of `bs` (he `start` and `end` arguments
can be used to focus on a sub-range):

```python
bs = b"".join((snapshot.latest_read for snapshot in snapshots))[start:end]
pos = snapshots[0].latest_read_start+start
```

Th utility function returns one or two lines of error message, based on that information:

1. The first line shows (a selection of) the bytes read and the position of the first byte in the stream
2. If `details` is specified, the second line highlights a selection of bytes from the first line, followed by the given details

The following optional keyword arguments can be used to customise the selection of read bytes and the highlighting.

- The `pad_start` arguments can be used to specify whitespace padding at the start of the bytes shown, to align them to bytes on other error lines.
- The `hl_start` and `hl_len` arguments can be used to specify the start byte and lenght of the range of bytes highlighted in the second line.
- The `dots` argument can be used to specify that three dots '...' should be added after the bytes, to indicate continuation.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils._decode_error_msg
documentation:
Creates a detailed, multi-line error message, starting from a given `msg` and taking into account the information from one or more stream snapshots.
The resulting error message has `msg` on the first line, followed by the lines returned by {func}`_decode_error_msg_lines`.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils._extract_error_cause_lines
documentation: Extracts lines of error description from a {class}`CBORDecodingError`.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding._err_utils._cid_error_template
documentation: Template for CID errors.

modules.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream
documentation: Byte-streams and snapshots used in DAG-CBOR decoding, keeping track of latest and previous read byte chunks for error reporting purposes.

classes.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.StreamSnapshot

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.StreamSnapshot.latest_read
documentation: The latest byte chunk read from the stream.

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.StreamSnapshot.latest_read_size
documentation: Size of the latest byte chunk read from the stream.

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.StreamSnapshot.latest_read_start
documentation: Start position in the stream for the latest byte chunk read.

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.StreamSnapshot.num_bytes_read
documentation: Total number of bytes read so far from the stream.
A snapshot of the current state of a byte-stream being decoded.

classes.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.Stream

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.Stream.curr_snapshot
documentation: A snapshot of the current state of the stream.

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.Stream.prev_snapshot
documentation: A snapshot of the state of the stream immediately before the latest non-extending read.

methods.name: dag_cbor.decoding._stream.Stream.read
documentation:
Read the given number of bytes from the stream. If {obj}`None`, reads all remaining bytes.
If `extend` is set to {obj}`True`, the current stream snapshot (see {attr}`Stream.curr_snapshot`) is extended with the bytes just read,
and the previous stream snapshot (see {attr}`Stream.prev_snapshot`) is kept.
Otherwise, the previous snapshot is replaced with the current snaptshot, and a new current snapshot is created with the bytes just read.
Container for the byte-stream being decoded, offering additional book-keeping functionality used to produce detailed error messages.

modules.name: dag_cbor.decoding
documentation: Deconding function for DAG-CBOR codec.

functions.name: dag_cbor.decoding.decode
documentation:
Decodes and returns a single data item from the given `stream_or_bytes`, with the DAG-CBOR codec.

A simple use for the optional `callback` argument is to count the number of bytes read from the stream:

```pycon
>>> import dag_cbor
>>> from io import BytesIO
>>> class BytesReadCounter:
...     _num_bytes_read = 0
...     def __call__(self, _, num_bytes_read):
...         self._num_bytes_read += num_bytes_read
...     def __int__(self):
...         return self._num_bytes_read
...
>>> encoded_bytes = b'\xa2aa\x0cabfhello!\x82\x00\x01'
>>> len(encoded_bytes)
16
>>> stream = BytesIO(encoded_bytes)
>>> bytes_read_cnt = BytesReadCounter()
>>> dag_cbor.decode(stream, allow_concat=True, callback=bytes_read_cnt)
{'a': 12, 'b': 'hello!'}
>>> int(bytes_read_cnt)
13
>>> bytes_remaining = stream.read()
>>> bytes_remaining
b'\x82\x00\x01'
>>> len(bytes_remaining)
3
>>> dag_cbor.decode(bytes_remaining)
[0, 1]
```

```
param stream_or_bytes

the bytes object or bytes stream to decode

param allow_concat

whether to allow partial stream decoding (if this is , a byte stream will always be consumed in its entirety)

param callback

optional callback to be invoked as callback(item, num_bytes_read) every time an item is decoded,
where num_bytes_read is the number of bytes read decoding the item (excluding sub-items, in the case of lists or dictionaries).

param require_multicodec

if , the data being decoded must be prefixed by the multicodec code for 'dag-cbor'
(see multicodec.unwrap).

param normalize_strings

whether strings should be normalised after decoding

raises CBORDecodingError

while reading the leading byte of a data item head, if no bytes are available

raises CBORDecodingError

while reading the argument bytes of a data item head,
if the expected number of argument bytes is not available

raises CBORDecodingError

while decoding the data of a bytestring or string, if the expected number of data bytes is not available

raises CBORDecodingError

while decoding the items of a list or a map (keys and values),
if the expected number of items is not available

raises CBORDecodingError

if an invalid utf-8 byte sequence is encountered while attempting to decode a string

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if attempting to decode the special  values NaN, Infinity and -Infinity

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if the additional info is greater than 27, or different from 27 for major type 7

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if an integer value was not minimally encoded

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if a key of a map is not a string

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if a map has repeated keys

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if map keys are not in canonical order

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if a tag (major type 6) different than 42 (for CID data) is encountered

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if non-bytestring data is found where CID data is expected (tag 42)

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if a simple value (major type 7) different from 20 (False), 21 (True) or 22 (None) is encountered

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if require_multicodec is set to  and
the bytes are not prefixed by the 'dag-cbor' multicodec code

raises DAGCBORDecodingError

if allow_concat is set to  and the decoding did not use all available bytes
```

modules.name: dag_cbor.random
documentation: Functions to generate random data.

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.reset_options
documentation: Resets random generation options to their default values.

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.default_options
documentation: Readonly view of the default random generation options.

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.get_options
documentation: Readonly view of the current random generation options.

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.options
documentation:
Returns with-statement context manager for temporary option setting:

```python
with options(**options):
    for value in rand_data(num_samples):
        ...
```

Options available:

```
seed: int           # set new random number generator, with this seed
min_int: int        # smallest `int` value
max_int: int        # largest `int` value
min_bytes: int      # min length of `bytes` value
max_bytes: int      # max length of `bytes` value
min_chars: int      # min length of `str` value
max_chars: int      # max length of `str` value
min_codepoint: int  # min utf-8 codepoint in `str` value
max_codepoint: int  # max utf-8 codepoint in `str` value
min_len: int        # min length of `list` and `dict` values
max_len: int        # max length of `list` and `dict` values
max_nesting: int    # max nesting of collections
canonical: bool     # whether `dict` values have canonically ordered keys
min_float: float    # smallest `float` value
max_float: float    # largest `float` value
float_decimals: int # number of decimals to keep in floats
include_cid: bool   # whether to generate CID values
```

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.set_options
documentation: Permanently sets random generation options. See {func}`options` for the available options.

functions.name: dag_cbor.random.rand_data
documentation:
Generates a stream of random data.

```
param n

the number of samples to be yielded; if , an infinite stream is yielded

param max_nesting

the maximum nesting level for containers; if , value from  is used
```

Maximum nesting level for containers:

- the integer value -1 means no containers will be generated
- integer values >= 0 mean that containers will be generated, with items generated by `random_data(max_nesting=max_nesting-1)`
- no other values are valid

modules.name: dag_cbor.ipld
documentation: Types and functions relating to the IPLD data model `IPLD data model <https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/>`_.

functions.name: dag_cbor.ipld._access
documentation: Implementation for {func}`IPLDObjPath.access` and {func}`IPLDObjPath.__rshift__`.

variables.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDScalarKind
documentation:
Python type alias for scalar [kinds](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/) in the IPLD data model:

- {obj}`None` for the [Null kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#null-kind)
- {obj}`bool` for the [Boolean kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#boolean-kind)
- {obj}`int` for the [Integer kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#integer-kind)
- {obj}`float` for the [Float kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#float-kind)
- {obj}`str` for the [String kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#string-kind)
- {obj}`bytes` for the [Bytes kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#bytes-kind)
- {class}`CID` for the [Link kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#link-kind)

variables.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDKind
documentation:
Python type alias for [kinds](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/) in the IPLD data model:

- {obj}`None` for the [Null kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#null-kind)
- {obj}`bool` for the [Boolean kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#boolean-kind)
- {obj}`int` for the [Integer kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#integer-kind)
- {obj}`float` for the [Float kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#float-kind)
- {obj}`str` for the [String kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#string-kind)
- {obj}`bytes` for the [Bytes kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#bytes-kind)
- {class}`CID` for the [Link kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#link-kind)
- {obj}`List` for the [List kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#list-kind)
- {obj}`Dict` for the [Map kind](https://ipld.io/docs/data-model/kinds/#map-kind)

variables.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPathSegment
documentation:
An individual segment in a {class}`IPLDObjPath` within a IPLD value (see {obj}`IPLDKind` for the ). A segment can be an {obj}`int` or a {obj}`str`:

- an {obj}`int` segment is a position, indexing an item in a value of List {obj}`IPLDKind` (a {obj}`List` in Python)
- an {obj}`str` segment is a key, indexing a value in a value of Map {obj}`IPLDKind` (a {obj}`Dict` in Python)

variables.name: dag_cbor.ipld._IPLDObjPathSegments
documentation: Short type alias for multiple segments.

classes.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.parse
documentation:
Parses a {class}`IPLDObjPath` from a string representation where segments are separated by `"/"`, such as that returned by
{meth}`IPLDObjPath.__repr__`.

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath._new_instance
documentation: Returns an instance of {class}`IPLDObjPath` with given segments, without performing any validation.

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__new__
documentation: Constructor for {class}`IPLDObjPath`.

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.access
documentation:
Accesses the sub-value at this path in the given IPLD value.
Can be written more expressively as `self >> value`, see {meth}`IPLDObjPath.__rshift__`.

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__truediv__
documentation:
The `/` operator can be used to create paths by concatenating segments. Below we use `_` as a suggestive name for an empty path, acting as root:

```pycon
>>> _ = IPLDObjPath()
>>> p = _/2/'red'
>>> p
/2/'red'
```

Concatenating an existing path with one or more segments returns a new path, extended by the given segments:

```pycon
>>> p/3
/2/'red'/3
>>> p/0/'blue'
/2/'red'/0/'blue'
```

Concatenating two paths yields a new path, where the end of the first path is treated as the root for the second:

```pycon
>>> q = _/0/'blue'
>>> p/q
/2/'red'/0/'blue'
```

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__rtruediv__
documentation:
It is possible to prepend a single segment at a time to an existing path using `/` (a new path is returned):

```pycon
>>> _ = IPLDObjPath()
>>> p = _/2/'red'
>>> 1/p
/1/2/'red'
```

Prepending multiple segments requires brackets (because the `/` operator associates to the left):

```pycon
>>> 0/(1/p)
/0/1/2/'red'
```

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__le__
documentation:
The `<` and `<=` operators can be used to check whether a path is a (strict) sub-path of another path, starting at the same root:

```pycon
>>> _ = IPLDObjPath()
>>> p = _/0/'red'
>>> q = p/1/2
>>> p == q
False
>>> p <= q
True
>>> p < q
True
```

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__lt__
documentation: See {meth}`IPLDObjPath.__le__`.

methods.name: dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__repr__
dag_cbor.ipld.IPLDObjPath.__rshift__
documentation:
Accesses the sub-value at this path in the given IPLD value:

```pycon
>>> _ = IPLDObjPath()
>>> _ >> [0, False, {"a": b"hello", "b": "bye"}]
[0, False, {'a': b'hello', 'b': 'bye'}]
>>> _/2 >> [0, False, {"a": b"hello", "b": "bye"}]
{'a': b'hello', 'b': 'bye'}
>>> _/2/'b' >> [0, False, {"a": b"hello", "b": "bye"}]
'bye'
```

Path within an object of {obj}`IPLDKind`, as a sequence of {obj}`IPLDObjPathSegment`.
Paths are immutable and hashable, and a path is a {obj}`Sequence` of the segments that constitute it.
